content:
# Cryptocrystalline

**Summary**: A texture where crystals are too small to be seen with a standard microscope.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] characterized the cryptocrystalline nature of the magnesite samples.

## Scientific Context

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] Determining mineral phase distribution in fine-grained rocks.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] Petrographic microscopy and SEM.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Microscopic study revealed the cryptocrystalline and microcrystalline nature of most magnesite samples. ([[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985, p. 31]])
